what_this_enables: Create custom data fields on any CRM object to capture business-specific information that HubSpot's default properties don't cover.

location_in_hubspot.navigation: Settings (gear) > Data Management > Properties > Create property

use_cases: Capture free-form short answers like external IDs or reference codes
Store URLs or links not covered by default fields
Record short notes or labels specific to your business process
Track external system identifiers for integration mapping
constraints: Maximum 65,536 characters

use_cases: Capture detailed notes or descriptions that exceed single-line length
Store multi-paragraph context like implementation notes or requirements
Record free-form feedback or qualification notes from sales calls
constraints: Maximum 65,536 characters

use_cases: Track counts like number of employees, locations, or licenses
Store financial figures like budget or ARR
Record quantities for sizing or capacity planning
Capture scores from external systems
constraints: Supports decimals
No currency formatting by default

use_cases: Standardize categorical data like industry vertical or product interest
Create picklists for rep-entered qualification fields
Build filterable segments based on fixed option sets
Control data quality by restricting input to predefined values
Enable consistent reporting across teams
constraints: Single selection only
Options can be added later but internal values cannot be changed

use_cases: Track multiple product interests or feature requirements simultaneously
Record multi-select qualifications like compliance certifications held
Capture multiple communication preferences
constraints: Values stored as semicolon-separated string in API
Options can be added but internal values are immutable

use_cases: Binary or small-set choices displayed as radio buttons on forms
Lifecycle or stage fields where only one value applies
Yes/No/Maybe style qualification questions
constraints: Single selection only
Renders as radio buttons on forms, dropdown in record view

use_cases: Track opt-in/opt-out flags like 'Agreed to NDA' or 'Marketing consent'
Create binary qualifiers like 'Is decision maker' or 'Has budget'
Flag records for specific processing like 'Needs review' or 'VIP'
Track completion states like 'Onboarding complete'
constraints: Values are 'true' or 'false' (strings in API)
Defaults to false if not set

use_cases: Track contract renewal dates or subscription expiry
Record milestone dates like 'Go-live date' or 'Onboarding start'
Capture date-only events where time of day is irrelevant
Set future action dates for workflow triggers
constraints: Date only, no time component
Stored as midnight UTC timestamp

use_cases: Store additional phone numbers beyond the default phone and mobile fields
Capture department-specific numbers like 'Direct line' or 'Office phone'
Track international numbers with formatting
constraints: No automatic formatting or validation
Stored as plain string

use_cases: Derive values from other properties (e.g., days since last contact)
Compute ratios or percentages from two numeric fields
Concatenate string fields into a formatted display value
Calculate time differences between date properties
constraints: Value is always derived, never manually set
Formula references other properties by internal name
Recalculated automatically when source properties change

use_cases: Build lead scoring models based on weighted property values
Create engagement scores combining multiple activity signals
Compute fit scores from firmographic data points
Prioritize records with composite quality scores
constraints: Value is always derived from scoring rules
Cannot be manually overridden
Score recalculates when source properties change
Requires HubSpot Marketing Hub Professional or higher for full scoring

use_cases: Aggregate values from associated records (e.g., total deal amount on a company)
Count associated records meeting certain criteria
Compute min/max/average across associated object properties
constraints: Rolls up from associated objects only
Limited aggregation functions: sum, count, min, max, average

use_cases: Estimate reading time for content-heavy properties or associated content
constraints: Specialized calculation type
Limited use outside content contexts

use_cases: Store formatted notes with bold, italics, and lists
Capture rich-text descriptions or instructions
Record detailed implementation notes with structure
constraints: Stored as HTML
Rendered with formatting in record view
Not searchable by content in lists

global_constraints: Internal name cannot be changed after creation
Internal name auto-populates from label (lowercased, spaces converted to underscores)
Property names are case-sensitive in API contexts
Calculation properties cannot be manually overridden

dropdown_vs_calculation.use_dropdown_when: The value needs to be manually selected or set by a workflow, and the set of options is known upfront.
dropdown_vs_calculation.use_calculation_when: The value should always be derived from other properties and never manually overridden.

number_vs_score.use_number_when: You need a raw count, amount, or quantity that can be manually entered or set via workflow.
number_vs_score.use_score_when: You need a weighted composite of multiple inputs that auto-recalculates — typically for lead scoring or prioritization.

date_vs_datetime.use_date_when: Only the calendar day matters (e.g., contract renewal date, go-live date).
date_vs_datetime.use_datetime_when: The specific time of day matters (e.g., meeting timestamp, SLA deadline with hours).

text_vs_dropdown.use_text_when: Input is free-form, unpredictable, or unique per record (e.g., external IDs, URLs, notes).
text_vs_dropdown.use_dropdown_when: Input should be constrained to a fixed set of options for consistency and reporting (e.g., industry, region, product tier).
